name: sslens
description: Modern SSL/TLS certificate inspector & pinning toolkit with GitHub Copilot integration. Fetch, analyze, and export certificates with ease. Perfect for mobile developers.

chatParticipants.name: sslens
chatParticipants.description: Inspect SSL/TLS certificates, check expiry, generate pinning code

commands.name: check
commands.description: Fetch and analyze the certificate chain of a live host

commands.name: pin
commands.description: Generate certificate pinning code for a host (OkHttp, URLSession, Alamofire, Dio, …)

commands.name: expiry
commands.description: Certificate expiry report for the given domains, or all saved domains

commands.name: compare
commands.description: Compare the certificates of two hosts

languageModelTools.name: sslens_inspectCertificate
languageModelTools.userDescription: Fetch and inspect the live SSL/TLS certificate chain of a host
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Connects to a host over TLS and returns its full certificate chain as JSON: subject, issuer, validity dates, days until expiry, public key algorithm and size, SHA-256/SHA-1 fingerprints, SPKI pinning hashes, subject alternative names, and chain trust status. Use this whenever the user asks about the SSL/TLS certificate, HTTPS setup, expiry, fingerprints, or pinning hashes of a specific domain.

domain.description: Hostname or URL to inspect, e.g. 'api.example.com' or 'https://example.com'

languageModelTools.name: sslens_generatePinningCode
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate certificate pinning code for a mobile platform using live SPKI hashes
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetches the live certificate of a host, computes SPKI pinning hashes, and returns ready-to-use certificate pinning code for a mobile platform. Supported platforms: 'android-okhttp', 'android-retrofit', 'ios-swift' (URLSession), 'ios-alamofire', 'flutter-dio', 'flutter-http', 'react-native'. Use when the user wants certificate/SSL pinning code for an app.

platform.description: Target platform/HTTP client for the generated code

includeChainHashes.description: Also include intermediate/root SPKI hashes as backup pins (recommended)

languageModelTools.name: sslens_checkExpiry
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check SSL certificate expiry dates for one or more hosts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Checks the SSL/TLS certificate expiry for up to 25 hosts in one call and returns, per host: expiry date, days until expiry, issuer, and a status of 'ok', 'expiring-soon' (30 days or less) or 'expired'. Use for questions like 'are my certs about to expire?' or monitoring several domains at once.

domains.description: Hostnames to check, optionally with ports ('example.com' or 'example.com:8443')

languageModelTools.name: sslens_listSavedDomains
languageModelTools.userDescription: List the domains saved in the SSLens sidebar
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the user's saved (favorite) domains from the SSLens sidebar, including alias, port, last-checked timestamp and last known certificate fingerprint. Use this to find out which domains the user monitors before running expiry checks or inspections on 'my domains'.

commands.title: SSLens: Ask Copilot About a Certificate

walkthroughs.title: Get started with SSLens
walkthroughs.description: Inspect certificates, generate pinning code, and use SSLens with GitHub Copilot.

steps.title: Fetch your first certificate
steps.description:
Fetch and inspect the full certificate chain of any HTTPS host.
[Fetch Certificate](command:sslens.fetchCertificate)

steps.title: Save domains you care about
steps.description:
Keep the domains you monitor one click away in the SSLens sidebar.
[Open SSLens Sidebar](command:workbench.view.extension.sslens-container)

steps.title: Generate pinning code
steps.description:
Produce ready-to-ship certificate pinning code for OkHttp, URLSession, Alamofire, Dio, and more.
[Generate Pinning Code](command:sslens.generatePinningCode)

steps.title: Chat with @sslens in Copilot
steps.description:
Ask @sslens to check, compare, or pin certificates in natural language — or let Copilot agent mode call SSLens tools automatically.
[Open Copilot Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%5B%22%40sslens%20%2Fcheck%20github.com%22%5D)

sslens.defaultPort.description: Default port to use when fetching certificates

sslens.maxRecentItems.description: Maximum number of recent certificates to keep

sslens.showExpiryWarnings.description: Show warnings for certificates expiring within 30 days

sslens.autoRefresh.description: Automatically refresh saved domains on startup

scripts.package-build: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.mjs --production
